prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sweetheart, a CPU is like the little brain inside the computer. I picture it as a tiny, busy worker inside that box, thinking really fast and helping the computer know what to do—like when you type your name or click on a game. It doesn’t see or hear, of course, but it remembers things for a short time and figures out how to make everything run smooth. Kind of like how I follow a recipe to bake your favorite cookies—step by step. I don’t know just where it is inside that machine, but I’m told it’s very important, like the heart or the head of the whole thing. Without it, the computer wouldn’t wake up or do a single thing!
